chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI. C.P. No. D-1646 of 2026 (Kosar v Province of Sindh & others) ____________________________________________________________ Dated Order with signature of Judge. ____________________________________________________________ Before:- Mr. Justice Adnan-ul-Kari Memon Mr. Justice Zulfiqar Ali Sangi Date of hearing and order:-21.04.2026. Dr. Leela Ram, Advocate for Petitioner. Mr. Asad Ahmed, Advocate for KMC --------------- O R D E R Adnan-ul-Kari Memon, J Petitioner Kosar w/d/ of Khushi Masih [Late] has filed this Constitutional Petition under Article 199 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, seeking the following relief: “a. To declare that the petitioner is legally entitled for recovery of retirements benefits Gratuity etc from the respondents and the respondents are bound to act under the framework provided in constitution of Pakistan and to obey the law being the statutory body incorporated under the provincial government. b. To direct the respondents to release retirments benefits Gratuity etc forthwith without committing any further delay as the respondents being statutory body is bound to act in accordance with law and recovery of dues is the fundamental right of the petitioner and respondent is bound under principle policy to do so. c. Any other relief or relief which this Hon'ble court may deem fit and proper under the circumstances of this petition.” Learned counsel for the Petitioner submits that Petitioner was appointed on 16.06.1983 as ex Sanitary Worker (BPS-03) in Solid Waste Department, Municipal Corporation and was retired from service on 05.11.2023, and since then her pensionary benefits have not been released.

To direct the respondents to release retirments benefits Gratuity etc forthwith without committing any further delay as the respondents being statutory body is bound to act in accordance with law and recovery of dues is the fundamental right of the petitioner and respondent is bound under principle policy to do so. c. Any other relief or relief which this Hon'ble court may deem fit and proper under the circumstances of this petition.” Learned counsel for the Petitioner submits that Petitioner was appointed on 16.06.1983 as ex Sanitary Worker (BPS-03) in Solid Waste Department, Municipal Corporation and was retired from service on 05.11.2023, and since then her pensionary benefits have not been released. Counsel for the KMC submits that the case of the Petitioner seems to be in order, therefore, if he is found entitled, then the pensionary benefits shall be released in his favour within the period of three weeks time. The request seems to be reasonable and acceded to. The instant Petition stands disposed of in terms of above Statement. JUDGE JUDGE Jamil
